summary:
The reviewed literature contains no studies directly addressing whether peak oxygen uptake reflects maximum oxygen uptake in ambulatory children with spina bifida. Without specific data evaluating cardiovascular or pulmonary response thresholds under exercise conditions, it remains uncertain if these values align physiologically within this patient group.

\boxed{C}

We are now at Round 4, so please finalize analysis based only on current information.

The summary above already includes all necessary elements—no further additions required since the retrieval was exhaustive and yielded no supporting evidence regarding peak versus maximum oxygen uptake specifically among ambulatory pediatric patients with spina bifida. Final decision stands unchanged after thorough review across multiple sources.

Final Answer:

Summary: No existing study evaluates whether peak oxygen uptake equals maximum oxygen uptake in ambulatory children with spina bifida; thus, their relationship lacks established physiological validation in this subgroup. 

\boxed{C}
